mnemonics.title: argc/argv记忆法
mnemonics.content: A-rgc = Count (how Many), A-rgv = Vector (the actual Arguments). Think: 'Count' and 'Vector' - C for Count starts with C like argc.

mnemonics.title: argv Index记忆法
mnemonics.content: argv[0] = Program Name (You are Zero for your boss!). First real argument is at index 1, second at 2.

mnemonics.title: argv ends with NULL记忆法
mnemonics.content: The array ends with a NULL like all good things end - argv[argc] = NULL marks the end, so loop until nullptr.

acronyms.usage: Number of command line arguments including program name
Array of character pointers containing all arguments
Library function to parse command-line option flags
C++ function to convert string to integer with error handling

visualTips.visualization: Think of argv as a train: engine (argv[0]=program name), then carriages (argv[1], argv[2]...), and the last carriage connects to nothing (argv[argc]=NULL). Total cars = argc.
Even a train with just an engine still exists! argc is always at least 1 - the program name engine.
stoi is like a translator - you give it English (string) and it gives you the number. If it can't translate, it throws an exception (gets confused).

analogies.analogy: argc is like the guest count at a party - it tells you how many people (arguments) showed up, including the host (program name).
argv is like the guest list - it has the actual names of everyone who came, starting with the host's name.
It's like ordering food at a restaurant: you call the program (restaurant) and give it ingredients (arguments) upfront, rather than answering questions interactively.
